item.title: How To Infiltrate Windmere Manor In Crimson Desert (Bloodied Invitation Quest)
item.description:
You need to infiltrate the Windmere manor in Crimson Desert to bring Count Byron to justice. You must go through his guards and reach his chamber to assassinate him. Doing this will complete the Bloodied Invitation quest and bring Kliff closer to his goal. This guide will share more information on how to infiltrate the Windmere and assassinate Count Byron in Crimson Desert .

How To Infiltrate Windmere Manor And Assassinate Count Byron

To infiltrate the Windmere manor, you need to find a blind spot where the guards can't detect Kliff. The best place to start the infiltration is from the back of the manor, at its northern side.

Jump at the manor's roof, targeting the two windows in the back. Opening both windows works, as they both lead to the same chamber. While trying to open the window and enter the Windmere manor, some guards might walk underneath the windows. Make sure to wait until they're gone to try again. Just climb the roof, crouch, and wait until they're gone.

After opening the window, you can access the chamber where you can immediately locate Count Byron. It may be clunky to go through the window, so keep trying. It's going to work eventually.

He won't detect Kliff as he's not looking at the windows, so it's better to stay in stealth mode. After that, approach him and press the button to assassinate him. If you walk inside instead of crouching, Kliff will be captured, and you'll have to infiltrate the Windmere manor again.

After infiltrating Windmere Manor and assassinating Count Byron, you can aim your lantern at the center of the room to learn a new memory and play it. It's the memory of Byron Manor, where Kliff learns more about his alliance with Myurdin.

Crimson Desert

item.title: Resident Evil 3 Remake Is the Lowest It Has Ever Been on Steam Right Now
item.description:
Capcom is celebrating Resident Evil ’s 30th anniversary by slashing the price of Resident Evil 3 remake on Steam to its lowest ever. Since its launch on April 3, 2020, Resident Evil 3 has sparked a wide range of reactions from players, particularly when compared to the 1999 original. While some fans noted differences in pacing and design choices, others appreciated the remake for its modernized approach and updated presentation.

Following the massive success of the Resident Evil 2 remake in 2019, expectations were naturally high for Capcom’s reimagining of the story of Resident Evil 3 . The game offers a more streamlined experience, with a stronger focus on action and cinematic storytelling. While certain elements were reworked or shortened, the remake still delivers a polished action horror experience with impressive visuals and refined gameplay mechanics.

Resident Evil 3 Remake Is Available for a Steal Right Now

Thanks to the ongoing Steam Spring Sale, players can grab some amazing games at an affordable price. The remake of Resident Evil 3 on Steam for just $3.99, bringing the title down to its lowest-ever price, offering a 90% discount on its original price of $39.99, valid until March 26. Since the game’s DLCs are also discounted, players can pick them up to access extra content like additional costumes for Jill and Carlos, the original Resident Evil 3 soundtrack, and unlocks for all in-game rewards.

Considering that the Resident Evil 3 remake is quite short when compared to other games in the franchise, $3.99 seems like a steal considering the replayability and in-game challenges it offers. Fans of other games from the Resident Evil franchise can also find similar discounts on other titles from the series, including Resident Evil 4, 5, and 6 .

Resident Evil 3 Remake Deserves Another Chance

The Resident Evil 3 remake is about to have its sixth anniversary in April. Since its release, many gamers have shared mixed feedback, especially when comparing it to the original version. However, over the last few years, many fans have come to appreciate the game for what it brings to the table in terms of its visuals and gameplay mechanics.

Drag weapons to fill the grid

Resident Evil 3 remake delivers an action-packed storyline, allowing players to jump straight into Raccoon City and relive the modern reimagining of the 1999 classic. The game currently sits with a comfortable Very Positive rating on Steam, making it a must-play if you are a fan of the franchise. With the game on sale right now, there couldn't be a better time to try the Resident Evil 3 remake.

7 /10

Resident Evil 3

item.title: How to Get & Use the Golden Piggy Bank in Crimson Desert
item.description:
Players can accumulate infinite wealth in Crimson Desert thanks to the Golden Piggy Bank, a key item found in a side quest that will reward your inventory with coin purses, even when you're offline. It's an incredible feature that you need to take advantage of.

Players can get the Golden Piggy Bank by defeating Walter Lanford , a boss in Crimson Desert who appears during the House Serkis Faction Questline in Hernand. Below, we'll detail how to get this quest and how to use the Golden Piggy Bank.

How to Get & Use the Golden Piggy Bank in Crimson Desert

  1. Complete House Serkis Faction Quests in Hernand.
  2. We completed 10 House Serkis quests before we got "The Nemesis' Demise" , which rewards the Golden Piggy Bank.
  3. If you've completed House Serkis quests and you don't have "The Nemesis' Demise", then complete other Faction Quests in Hernand, or liberate camps from Bleed Bandits.
  4. The Golden Piggy Bank is rewarded by defeating Walter Lanford , the boss fight during "The Nemesis' Demise".
  5. To use the Golden Piggy Bank, enter your inventory and select "Key Items", where you can find this item waiting.
  6. Using the Golden Piggy Bank will return you to the title screen .
  7. The next time you log in, you will have hundreds of Light Copper Pouches , depending on how long you were offline for.

Below, we'll detail how you can easily defeat Walter Lanford, the shotgun menace, so you can get the Golden Piggy Bank without too much trouble.

How to Defeat Walter Lanford in Crimson Desert

  1. Walter Lanford utilizes a shotgun, so he's going to try to keep his distance with dashes and smoke grenades to shoot you from a distance.
  2. Use Blinding Flash Finishers (L1+R1+R2) to close the gap on Walter.
  3. Utilize dodges instead of parries against Walter, as you won't be able to parry his shotgun pellets.
  4. Bring plenty of healing items so that you can outheal the damage that Walter deals.
  5. Walter's only a one-phase boss, and he shouldn't be too much trouble if you're appropriately leveled for his encounter.

Crimson Desert

item.title: How To Infiltrate Sungrove Manor In Crimson Desert (Whispering Shadows Quest)
item.description:
To infiltrate the Sungrove manor in Crimson Desert , you have to get on top of the manor. From there, find a window to get access inside. There are two memories that Kliff needs to learn and play inside the Sungrove manor, so this guide will give more information on how to infiltrate the Sungrove manor and read the memories in Crimson Desert .

How To Infiltrate Sungrove Manor In Crimson Desert

You can infiltrate the Sungrove manor by flying to its roof. It's better to jump from the backside, where there are no guards. Aim to land on the right side of the manor, since the window to enter the manor is from that side.

Descend to one of the windows on the right side and open it. It may be clunky to open the window as the button prompt won't appear. Try to wait a bit before you move again since the button prompt needs time to appear.

Enter the window to get inside the chamber that has the first memory. You'll face the same clunkiness when trying to enter through the window. So, it's better not to wait for a button prompt to appear. Instead, place Kliff underneath the window, then climb it, and the animation will prioritize getting Kliff inside the window.

Aim your lantern at the desk, and it should read the memory on Beatrice Azerian's whereabouts.

Exit the room and take the stairs down to the first floor.

There should be a guard there. Take them out as quickly as possible before they alert the other guards. It's also important to take them out since the memory is where they're standing. It's not that easy to learn and play the memory when a guard is attacking Kliff.

Finally, after taking out the guard, aim the lantern at the center of the corridor, and that should allow Kliff to learn and play the memory on Beatrice's co-conspirators. Infiltrating the Sungrove manor will complete the Whispering Shadows quest and start the Bloodied Invitation quest in Crimson Desert .

Crimson Desert

item.title: How To Repair The Pensive Statues In Crimson Desert (Jijeong Temple In Chaos Quest)
item.description:
To repair the pensive statues in Crimson Desert , you must find the parts of the pensive statues and assemble them on the statue's platform. Out of the three puzzles, two of them require you to place two hands on the statue, while the other one also needs you to fix the pensive statue's body.

How To Repair The Pensive Statues In Crimson Desert

Before attempting to do the Jijeong Temple in Chaos quest in Crimson Desert , it's best to unlock the two marked waypoints first. The Abyss Nexus is near the entrance to the Serpent Marsh, whereas the Abyss Cresset is an open Cresset that you can interact with without doing any puzzles.

Once you're ready, head to the marked area, and you should start seeing hand symbols on the map. Approach it to see a broken stone hand.

Use the Axiom Force to grab the hand and carry it to the pensive statue. Here, you may be tempted to align the hand manually, but you don't have to.

Instead, use Force Current to snap the hand in place when there's a green light around the hand . The green light means that the game registers the input, and you can snap it in without having to do proper alignment.

The second pensive statue is trickier. Grab the statue and hover it on top of the platform until the green light appears. Let go of the statue, then climb up.

From atop the statue, aim the Axiom Force down, then use the Force Current to push it down and snap it back in place.

One of the hands for the second pensive statue is down the cliff, but you can still grab it without going down using your Axiom Force hook.

The final pensive statue has a hand that must be taken from another cliffside.

Take it, then release it near where the statue is. The hand will float, so don't worry about it falling.

Then do the same step where you snap the hand in place when there's a green light.

Now, you must report back to the Chief Monk, but they're not at the temple anymore. Instead, you must find them on top of one of the isles.

Climb up on the marked spot and talk to the Chief Monk to complete the Jijeong Temple in Chaos quest.

The next mission is to complete three trials all over Serpent Marsh. Once that's done, get ready for a boss fight at the end of the questline.

Crimson Desert
